items.title: Public service watchdog calls for $14M budget increase amid 'steep' climb in complaints
items.summary_auto: Public Sector Integrity Commissioner Harriet Solloway says the integrity and whistleblowing system will struggle to function without a funding boost.

items.title: Buyers emerge for Hudson's Bay stores in downtown Vancouver, Calgary, Ottawa: court docs
items.summary_auto: Buyers have been found for four properties that used to be home to Hudson's Bay department stores, including Vancouver's Granville Street location.

items.title: Man charged with 1st-degree murder in deaths of 2 children found in northwest Calgary
items.summary_auto: A man has been charged with two counts of first-degree murder in the deaths of two children found in a vehicle in northwest Calgary on Thursday.

items.title: James Chiu, president and co-founder of iconic Mandarin restaurant chain, dead at 78
items.summary_auto: James Chiu died on Wednesday just days after his 78th birthday after a battle with pulmonary fibrosis, his family said.

items.title: 'I wanted to protect him': Mother mourns teen slain near school amid settler violence in West Bank
items.summary_auto: More than a week after a shooting near a boys' school in a West Bank village left a teenage boy and a man in his 30s dead, the community remains on edge, and residents say they fear more violence at the hands of Israeli settlers and the military.

items.title: 4 killed in Israeli strikes in south Lebanon as Hezbollah drone wounds 2 Israeli soldiers
items.summary_auto: Israel carried out several airstrikes in southern Lebanon on Friday, killing at least four people, while the militant Hezbollah group said it fired rockets and drones, including one that crashed in northern Israel and wounded two soldiers.

items.title: Feds to quadruple max fine for airlines violating air passenger bill of rights to $1M
2 police officers to stand trial in highway crash east of Toronto that killed baby, grandparents
items.summary_auto: Two Durham police officers will stand trial in connection with a multi-vehicle collision in April 2024 that killed four people — including two grandparents and their infant grandchild — after police chased a suspect driving the wrong way on Canada’s busiest highway.

items.title: Man accused in Edmonton police officers' deaths being scapegoated: defence
items.summary_auto: Court of King's Bench Justice John Little heard closing arguments Thursday in the case against a 21-year-old man who faces more than a dozen charges from the shooting that claimed the lives of Const. Travis Jordan and Const. Brett Ryan.

items.title: Scam QR codes are showing up on Bike Share bicycles, parking machines. Here's what to look for
items.summary_auto: A sneaky scam using false QR codes is targeting those using Bike Share bikes and parking machines in Mississauga.

items.title: Pipe dream? Majority polled in Alberta don’t see new pipeline becoming a reality
items.summary_auto: Despite the fanfare that accompanied its signing, a majority polled by Janet Brown Opinion Research for CBC News are not confident the memorandum of understanding between Prime Minister Mark Carney and Premier Danielle Smith will actually result in a new pipeline.

items.title: Would Manitoba's social media ban actually protect kids?
items.summary_auto: Manitoba Premier Wab Kinew says he wants to ban social media and artificial intelligence chatbots for youth. But would this plan keep youth healthier and safer? CBC reporter Bryce Hoye investigates.

items.title: Will the MOU result in a pipeline from Alberta? Most polled don’t think so
items.summary_auto: Despite the fanfare that accompanied its signing, a majority polled by Janet Brown Opinion Research for CBC News are not confident the memorandum of understanding between Prime Minister Mark Carney and Premier Danielle Smith will actually result in a new pipeline.

items.title: 1 year later, few answers: the 'profoundly rare' disappearance of Lilly and Jack Sullivan
items.summary_auto: In a country where most missing children are found within days, Lilly and Jack Sullivan’s case stands out: two young siblings, disappearing at the same time in a way investigators still can’t explain.

items.title: N.B. nuclear company selling ‘distressed assets’ to B.C. buyer
items.summary_auto: A company that was aiming to build its first small modular nuclear reactor in New Brunswick is putting some of its assets up for sale as its future in the province is increasingly in doubt.

items.title: Protesters clash with police in Australia after suspected killer of Indigenous girl arrested
items.summary_auto: Hundreds of protesters clashed with Australian emergency services workers in a remote town following the arrest of a man suspected ‌of murdering a five-year-old Indigenous girl, police said on Friday.

items.title: The U.S. Supreme Court's ruling on the Voting Rights Act could give Trump a lifeline
items.summary_auto: The conservative majority on the U.S. Supreme Court has effectively neutralized a hugely significant piece of voting rights legislation designed to ensure African-American representation in Washington, and the resulting consequences could be wide-reaching and election-changing.

items.title: How Quebec funds disadvantaged schools — and why some say it can hurt kids in need
items.summary_auto: For nearly 30 years, Quebec has used a system to determine which schools should get extra funding and help based on how disadvantaged they are compared to others. But critics say that system is flawed, outdated and can cause harm to young students.

items.title: How to survive if you’re suffering through Canada’s 'longer and more severe' allergy seasons
items.summary_auto: Seasonal allergies are getting worse in Canada, with longer and stronger pollen seasons, largely thanks to climate change. Here's a primer on the hardest-hit cities, the latest science and what treatments can help you survive another allergen-filled spring and summer.

items.title: Why a salary of $115K isn't enough to purchase a house in some parts of Canada
items.summary_auto: Thirty years ago, it was normal for a grocery store produce manager or part-time nurse to find the five per cent downpayment needed to buy a home, says mortgage broker Ron Butler. Those days are gone. Now, someone earning $115,000 a year would have trouble buying a house in parts of Canada.

items.title: First Nations, Métis leaders critical of lack of distinctions-based funding in spring update
items.summary_auto: First Nations and Métis leaders are criticizing the federal government's spring economic update for a lack of distinctions-based funding for Indigenous communities in its multi-billion "Canada Strong" plan.

items.title: Paging Dr. AI to the ER? Artificial intelligence shows promise in emergency room diagnosis
items.summary_auto: As hospitals increase their use of artificial intelligence to improve patient care, a U.S. study has found that an AI tool has proven effective at making emergency medical diagnoses — even better than actual doctors, in some cases.

items.title: Billionaire makes formal bid to relocate Vancouver Whitecaps to Las Vegas
items.summary_auto: A spokesperson for billionaire Grant Gustavson confirmed to CBC News he is fronting an investment group's bid for the team, with the intention to move them to Las Vegas.

items.title: 'Blatant recklessness': Saskatoon man faces jail time for fatal crash
items.summary_auto: Wylie Vermette, 25, pleaded guilty to dangerous driving causing the death of Natalie Gardipy in a crash that happened in Saskatoon on June 11, 2024. Lawyers recommended a sentence of two years in jail, but the judge said he needed some time to think about the case and will give his decision in June.

items.title: Defence chief set to pitch ‘options’ for massive troop surge as military strains under its own limits
Palestinian-Israeli tensions take centre stage at 76th FIFA Congress in Vancouver
items.summary_auto: Palestinian Football Federation president Jibril Rajoub refused to shake hands with his Israeli counterpart on stage at the Vancouver Convention Centre in front of hundreds of international soccer representatives.
